creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
Created attachment 230496
Highlights from the e-mail about recovering from a partially overwritten EFI system partition (ESP)

Kicking the ball around … 


Routine/preventative
====================

For suitable forms of wording, I suspect that we'll find numerous documents outside the freebds.org domain. Maybe mix and match, or cherry-pick one, to form the basis of something short, simple and user-friendly for the FreeBSD Handbook. 

Then update <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/faq/#disks> with a link to the relevant part(s) of the Handbook. 

In the meantime, inspiration maybe from <https://cgit.freebsd.org/src/commit/UPDATING?id=d8e59012e29dee301a7af5c2a70749637319fafc> (2020-10-31). 


Corrective
==========

As we have a subsection for troubleshooting _installations_ 
<https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/book/#bsdinstall-install-trouble>, so the section for _updating and upgrading_ <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/cutting-edge/> might benefit from a troubleshooting subsection. 

For now, this subsection could simply refer to, or draw from, Warner Losh's 
<https://lists.freebsd.org/archives/freebsd-current/2021-July/000331.html>


> Re: EFI boot partition overwritten

– screenshot attached, with highlights. 

My response <https://lists.freebsd.org/archives/freebsd-current/2021-July/000342.html> suggested a typo. I was wrong there (sorry!); the originally given command was valid.
